title: mentha × villosa var. wirtgeniana
intro: Mentha × villosa var. wirtgeniana, also known as Wirtgen's woolly mint, is a variety of hybrid mint that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to Europe, prized for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Mentha × villosa var. wirtgeniana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and topical applications, such as salves and creams, which can be made from its leaves and essential oil.
